examples: In March 2010, the new record for processing power goes to a Cray XT5 computer called Jaguar.
The company won a grant from America's Department of Energy to run simulations on Jaguar, a Cray XT-5 supercomputer at the Oak Ridge National Laboratory.
The Cray also, of course, runs Linux.
It's a humbling admission for Scott, a 19-year veteran of Cray with a Ph.
The first customer to sign a Cray XC30 contract was HLRS in Stuttgart back in 2010.
